channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 205 replies — 344.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 205 • Updated: 2026-07-14 16:43 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62473272
>>62473197
You the VA guy?

62473749
>>62473272
It’s the VA guy

62473786
Well, nevermind that shit. I just went to that store and saw who they hired instead of me. I don't know why I bothered.

62473888
It's the same shit as before, nobody even looks at my applications, nobody even talks to me.

62474038
>>62473888
you decided to look for jobs at the worst time, you retarded nigger

62474334
What are you supposed to do? Nothing you're told to do works.

62474529
>>62449961
just go sell cars. just go into sales. doesnt matter what

62474626
>>62451403
im in the same boat as you OP. start using your local resources. you said you live in the woods? start selling firewood. grow a bunch of plants and start a nursery and start selling plants. designer plants for decoration like japanese maples and shit. its a good side gig. make a website and shit for it then in a year or two when you apply for a real job say you were an entreprenuer and sold your business and want the security net of a regular 9-5 job now with the shitty economy. you can also just backdate this shit and fabricate a website and reviews for something that never happened too btw if you want to just start applying now withou getting any extra money or learning how business works

62474732
>>62450440
They don’t hire anyone retard

62474735
>>62452288
They
Don’t
Hire
You

62474751
even though I'm not a neet after being laid off I feel like I'm in a similar situation.
I have a leg up with my work experience and education but I don't feel like it was worth it which meant that I wasted my youth doing something even worse than just fucking around

62474795
to all the people in this situation who want actual sane advice, here it:

Do something easy. Something that won't demand a superior work ethic or a miserable grind with a manager looking at production numbers watching you.
You're likely gonna hate your life every single morning you wake up and have to work AND you didn't do this when you were 20 years old and it was easy. You can get over the hump and become a "worker" but the mornings are already your enemy, don't stack the odds further against you.

Work behind a register. Ideally at a gas station or some sleepy business. Maybe a long shot for you to get hired, but it's ideal. Some cleaning, some dealing with dickheads, some stocking. You won't be worked to the bone. A grocery/dollar store cashier is worse in most cases.

Inbound call centre. Yes you will hate your job but the work comes at you and your office should be air conditioned. If you have superior (compared to your coworkers) manners and people skills you may even find yourself promoted rapidly. 60 year old grannies get jobs in call centers and end up trainers and managers, you can do it.

Janitor. This one may be a stretch for a 40yo NEET but if you get the right gig, or hop into the right gig after getting a year experience, you may have found a cozy job. Many janitors are not running around like rats and barely have to deal with coworkers or the public. Maybe you end up hired by the local school board in half a year and make "decent" money.


If you really want to try your hand at job where you have a taskmaster watching you try these:

Warehouse worker.
Meatpacker.
Day laborer: Construction, landscaping, whatever.
Factory as a production worker. GL getting hired unless the job is literal hell with high turnover.
Outbound Call Centre

Good luck. If you show up every day for a 1 year or 2 a manager or senior coworker will likely give you a glowing reference. You can even become a real "worker" and have easier mornings in time.

62474821
>>62474795
None of those are hiring.

62474824
>>62474821
Then keep whining here like a bitch. Someone else will take the advice and make a few dollars. If a call centre won't take you, you may as well start picking up cans and bottles.

62474838
>>62474824
Just admit that you're wrong. There are no jobs.

62474844
>>62474795
if you're a man you don't exist. don't go to die in a dead end gas station. karma is real.

62474884
>>62449961
Anon, try asking an LLM (I recommend chat gpt but use whatever) on how to get a job. Explain your entire situation in as much detail as possible, why you haven't worked, depression, you're 40, never had a job, where you live (so it can recommend options) that you're carless.

Explain EVERYTHING. Learning how to utilize LLMs will really help you anyway. I'm getting close to 40 myself and I use LLMs daily (I am a professional with a good job not a NEET but it doesn't matter you need to use LLMs now even if you're employed). Anons ITT are mostly going to dunk on you because you're a huge fucking loser but the best time to turn your life around is right the fuck now. The LLM won't make you feel bad like everyone here is doing too.

Explain your interests to it too and maybe it can try and recommend a job you'll actually excel somewhat in, although beggars can't be choosy at this point.

62474892
>>62474884
the creature making the OP does not want advice. It's doing an act for (you)s
This is actually good advice to someone so defeated they can't find jobs felons on the sex offender registry get after 15 years binned up.
I'm sure a free AI could coach a defeated NEET into one of those jobs.
OP isn't though, just a weird fuck

62475051
>>62474884
I think this is the first time I've seen someone call Ai like chat gpt an "LLM". Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.

>>62474892
Get a life you fucking loser. Dogshit like you doesn't deserve even the attention I've given you already.

62475932
>>62475051
>Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.
Repeating myself, but the modern human needs to get comfortable using LLMs to be productive and successful. Avoid falling in to emotional or political traps involving them. View them as a useful tool. A human who refuses to use a calculator would be less productive than a human who does use a calculator.

The same is true for LLMs. Don't blindly trust everything it tells you. Get a feel for how to prompt it to give you the results you want and double check things that you aren't sure about. A lot of people on /biz/ and in general seethe over LLMs, call you a pajeet if you mention them etc, just ignore them. I'm a professional making 6 figs a year in an white collar position, I use LLMs every single day outside of work and inside of work.

As a skill-less NEET who doesn't know how to navigate the world it's the most invaluable tool at your disposal.

62475990
>>62449961
very white european of you

62476030
>>62475932
You put way too much faith in an algorithm poorly masquerading as ai. If it's a tool it should be a learning tool, not a crutch.

62476048
>>62476030
Nowhere did I say it's a crutch, I very clearly said it's a tool. You're starting to sound like you're beyond helping desu. Good luck.

62476501
>>62453688
The fact that you think there is any significant amount of “attacks” in this thread and not genuine criticism shows how far removed you are from the world. It’s going to be a tough couple years for you as you enter the workforce. You’ve received tons of honest advice.

62476747
>>62457849
Anon can we talk on private? I think I can help you with this job for a fraction of 5% of what you get paid. I am desperately in need of money right now.

62477511
OP is a mentally ill retard who is in denial about everything, or maybe just a troll.

ignore this thread.

62478296
i am in the exact same position as op, but i am 24 and have an undergrad in history
how do i even get a job, and i dont wanna work in a fucking school

62478683
>>62449961
I think the French Foreign legion will still take you.

Otherwise, you could save some McWagie dollars and go to a trade school. Plumbers, machinists, and other tech trades are in demand.

62479116
>>62478296
If you're this lost the anon who said "talk to chat GPT" had the correct advice for you. Scroll up and read it. An AI can easily guide you through the process of finding a starting job to build up. Any decent job wants to know you will show up every morning, gotta start low.
You can scroll up to my post, also. That advice applies to you. At 24 you will have more hope than a 40 year old though.
I would even recommend fast food at that age as you can actually fit in with the young folks who work at those jobs.
>>62478683
Trade school is a bust. If you are actually handy and been doing carpentry and repairing your own stuff since you were a teenager then you likely would have slid into this type of job already. Good luck getting some tradie boss to tolerate you if you don't know left from right. Seriously. Better off starting as a laborer and getting your hands dirty that way first, then moving into a trade.

62480858
What the hell kind of cleaner do you use on painted wood paneling?

item.title: Who's still buying BTC at this level? — 21 replies — 84.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who's still buying BTC at this level?

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-07-14 17:12 UTC

OP

BTC has already come a long way. Are you still buying here, waiting for a pullback, or just holding what you already have?

>Curious what everyone's plan is.

Early replies (first 3)

62475243
waiting for $10k and I will buy 3 btc

62475257
>>62475235
YOUR MOTHER

62475258
>>62475235
I'm DCAing bitcoin at the second half of the midterm year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62475365
>>62475257
Your grandmother

62475369
>>62475258
Keep buying big man

62475556
Still buying, 250k incoming

62475971
>>62475243
Waiting for 10k? Are you serious?

62476143
>>62475235
I DCA yes

62476227
>>62476143
do it everyday

62476239
>>62475258
It's scary how Ben is always right.

62476359
>>62476239
Ben is always right . Every day . All tweets are right

62476425
>>62475235
>Who's still buying BTC at this level?
One of the biggest corporation and all the billionaires in the world?
Also
ME?
On Top of all, there's 240,000 fucking BTC being traded everyday.
There's less than 944,000 Bitcoin to be mined left.
Make your math.

62477616
>>62476425
You're broke

62477948
>>62477616
What makes you think that?

62477951
>>62477616
I know him in real life, he's loaded.

62479061
>>62477951
Ben??

62479203
>>62475971
When 10k or 5k

62479569
>>62475235
Time to buy bitcoin

62480040
>>62479203
delusional peg

62480706
>>62477951
You are him.

62480931
im buying its the cheapest it will ever get!

62479968

>$7
and they lost thanks to Allah

62480032

Price bad

this is merely a fuddie setback

yeah pal, we know you're a linkie, you're not fooling anyone.

kys nigger.

62480175
But they did and still are

62480360

Fucker, you made me look it up.

62480413
>>62479946
It's happening, the first TradFi normies that work behind gov closed door got to witness the importance of Chainlink. They're the ones pumping it right now.
Expect god candles soon.

62480535
And the linkies shall inherit the earth

62480557
>>62479968
fuddies are mudslimes

>>62480535
Linkies are Christians

Glad to know I'm on the side of our Lord Jesus Christ. THE ONLY BEGOTTEN SON OF GOD THE FATHER, TOGETHER IN THE UNITY OF THE HOLY SPIRIT, GOD FOREVER! AMEN

62480569
>>62480557
>Linkies are Christians
No they are not.

62480605
>>62480557
Linkjeets are the worst filth ever. Third worlders pieces of shit and schizos who shilled for the 4th Industrial Revolution and the WEF Great Reset agenda

You'll will never make it and you will burn in hell along with other baggies

62480728
>>62480605
By the power of the Precious Blood, and in the Holy Name of Jesus, and with the intercession of the Blessed Virgin Mother, and with the intercession of St. Micheal the Archangel, I command you, demon of wickedness, to stop afflicting me, and to go to the foot of the cross and receive your sentence.

62480731
>>62480728
you're going to hell and you know it. Deep down you know you fucked up badly by shilling a Judeo-globohomo scam

62480915
>>62480557
linkies are godless heathens

item.title: Let me guess, you NEED more? — 29 replies — 36.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Let me guess, you NEED more?

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-07-14 16:31 UTC

OP

Let me guess, you NEED more?

Early replies (first 3)

62477627
Can I fuck the long haired pale ghost that lives in there?

62477639
>>62477619
...Bug infested dilapidated Shack on dirt...
>Bug infested dilapidated Shack on dirt in JAPAN

62477707
Correct.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62477721
>Renovations are legally required to keep up with modern building codes
>In the middle of nowhere surrounded by boomer japs who are tired of gaijin
>A ton of bureacratic hoops to jump through which is very hard if you're not fluent in Japanese or have a girlfriend/wife who is

62477750
>>62477639
S-SUGOI DESU NE

62477790
>>62477721
It's the opposite. The code in Japan is really forgiving for old derelict buildings. That's why the exist besides and old anti Samurai punitive tax for fallow land coming into play once you demolish.
You can renovate an existing structure ship of theseus style or you can live in squalor. But you can't demolish and you can't rebuild without the stuff you're referencing.

62477802
>>62477619
it's a scam, the government will rape you with additional fees for maintenance and disaster prevention shit or tell you to demolish it, these "3000" dollar houses either gonna cost you 500k in renovation or you'll be told to demolish it in 2 years because it's unsafe in case of an earthquake

62477809
>>62477790
Interesting. I could get a ski lodge for dirt cheap. Japan has incredible snow skiing from what I understand. I like japan.

62477821
>>62477721
Something similar in Canada would be 300-400k CAD

62477901
>>62477639
Cheaper than a trailer. And I do not have to live like a squatter until after the tourist visa runs out.

62478074
also aside from what others have said, rural japan is actually a very dangerous place
lots of kidnapping, human trafficking and all sorts of horrific third world tier crimes happen there

62478092
>>62478074
yes rural japan very dangerous. do not come

62478150
>>62477639
>>62477619
Wtf will my life be like Totoro

62478389
>>62477619
These things are ALL over rural Japan. If you want to live in a paper house with these crawling all around, by all means. Not to mention the insulation in those houses is non-existent.

62478394
>>62478389
do they bite? i bet they would go into your ear

62478471
>>62477639
/thread

62478493
>>62477619
In this clown world, life’s a relentless cycle of rejection, a cruel joke played on the weary soul. At 12, you're dismissed by employers—no chance to prove yourself, just discarded like trash. By 21, college turns you away, another door slammed shut, dreams dashed before they even bloom. At 30, love rejects you, leaving a hollow ache, as if you're invisible, unworthy. By 40, the military waves you off, too old, too broken, another badge of failure. In your 50s, even the parent of your washed-up daughter turns cold, rejecting what once was family, what once mattered. In your 60s, zoomers call you a pedo, mocking your existence, calling you invisible, irrelevant. By 70, retirement is just another rejection—no place for you, no purpose left. In your 80s, healthcare turns you away, an obstacle rather than aid. And in your 90s, caregivers abandon you, leaving you to fade into oblivion. There’s nothing to do in life but endure these rejections, a never-ending circus of despair. Welcome to clown world, where hope is a punchline and nothing truly matters.

62478529
>>62478493
>At 12, you're dismissed by employers
Damn, what kind loser is looking for work at 12?

62478539
>>62477802
Incels will come up with a million excuses why things can't be done

62478559
>>62478074
i don't know about the other stuff but japan actually had pretty weak human trafficking laws/enforcement up until a few years ago.
that stuff you see in anime and manga about people getting debt trapped and being forced to work in host and hostess clubs is real.

62478562
>>62477619
If you want to buy a cheap house, both China and Thailand is so much better than Japan.

In China you can get a house that’s less than 10 years old for under $5000 and it’s possibly the cheapest in the world to renovate

62478564
>>62477627
Only if you get it pregnant and produce lots of offspring...

62478567
>>62477619
what are you gonna do all day, except freeze to death in the winter?

62478569
>>62478389
that's why you just keep chickens...problem solved

62478624
>>62478529
You need to be 18yo to trade crypto.

62478660
>Get your house in the middle of nowhere smalltown Japan
>All the jobs pay like shit (there is a reason everyone moves to the city) and that's if your gaijin ass is even given a job to begin with
>By law the house has to be repaired by le 50 years experience craftsman that knows how to work on these old houses and will spend a hour planing a 1000 times folded 2x4 by hand on your dime
>Everyone will hate you now because of white retards on tiktok and the publicity surrounding Indians wanting in

62478679
>>62478394
they couldnt fit in your ear, unless your Dumbo.
>>62478389
>NOO!!!!
>NOT THE CARNIVOROUS INSECT THAT KILLS AND EATS 1000s of other insects!
Retards like you end up with a house full of flies and bugs.

62478792
0xE2DE18F2205612101782fc93d42f63620147b0E3 just give me 1000$ . thanks bigman!

62480807
>>62477619
you can't you have to live there

Early replies (first 3)

62480628
are we really going to WAGMI?

62480635

Indicies are going to moon

62480648
>>62480580

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62480896
>>62480580
I know Claver is probably full of shit but I really really want to believe. pic unrel

62480916
>>62480580
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62480922
This OP pic from two schizo threads ago is just to peak and funny not to post. If someone has the original WAGMI liminal please post.

62480923
if i posted about a memecoin that was released 10 minutes ago and said it was going to $2000, nobody would reply, yet there is literally a higher chance some memecoin goes to $2000 then XRP. Give it a rest faggots, it's over.

62480932

X POWER!!!!! BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

item.title: memes aside, is LINK in an accumulation zone below $8? — 17 replies — 19.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
memes aside, is LINK in an accumulation zone below $8?

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-07-14 16:27 UTC

OP

memes aside, is LINK in an accumulation zone below $8?

Early replies (first 3)

62475926
>>62475875
Token doesn't have value in long term so no.

62475929
p+c

62476525
>>62475875
it's going to $4 by december and it won't clear $20 in the next cycle

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62476532
if you want to accumulate poop, sure

62476558
I'm buying at $3 later this year

62476560
>>62475875
Memes aside?
Probably, maybe.

Realistically yes, all crypto is in an accumulation zone right now

62476582
>>62475875
All of crypto is in accumulation zone right now. The only reason I’m not buying is my bags were loaded up years ago, and I’m buying other things

62476591
Every time LINK goes low I really want to accumulate but I never do, and then a year later I'm always really, really glad that I didn't. So I'll just stick to that strategy

62477482
>>62475875
No. Unironically maybe below $2 which is where revenues actually make sense vs. subsidized network bloat. Still would not buy; there is zero actual source of demand no matter how successful CLL ever become.

62477810
>>62475875
No, not until they settle the equity overhang.

62477824
>>62475875
$4.50 and $7 is the sweet spot

62479355
>>62475875
You can buy this at $3 by the end of the year

62480689
>>62475875
bery good token kind sir please buy on binance now

62480741
>>62475875
It accumulates losers

62480748
>>62480741
bitch bloody bastard bulgarian

62480766
I'll buy at $2 early next year

62480790
>>62480748
I'll kill you for this

item.title: THE BIG SHART: IBM crashes 24% in 20 minutes after indian becomes CEO — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
THE BIG SHART: IBM crashes 24% in 20 minutes after indian becomes CEO

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-07-14 17:14 UTC

OP

From Zero Hedge:

>Wall Street Desks Stunned
IBM Shares Crash Most On Record, Exceeding Dot Com & 1987 Crashes
>CEO Arvind Krishna Blamed Preliminary 2Q Results on "Shifting" Customer CapEx Spending
>IBM's surprise second-quarter warning blindsided traders Tuesday morning, raising new concerns that enterprise technology budgets are being redirected toward AI infrastructure at the expense of traditional software and IT services.
Also, when you let brown people run a company, the company will crash. They say the company benchods from within
>Shares plunged 24% in the first 20 minutes of New York trading. Should those losses hold through the close, IBM would suffer its largest one-day crash on record, based on Bloomberg trading data going back to 1968.
Coolie runs white business and shits the punchbowl
>Here's what Wall Street's top desks are saying in first takes:
They're saying saaaaaaaaaaar
>UBS analyst Robert Ruple:
He got a diversity award
>The big news this morning was a surprising negative preannouncement by IBM, down 22%, with Q2 sales of $17.2 bn versus $17.8 bn expected and EPS of $2.93 versus $3.02. Citing unanticipated capex reprioritization impacting client buying patterns with numerous large deals failing to close on time, cybersecurity distractions and some supply chain-related impact where they saw clients shift their quarterly capex spend toward servers, storage, and memory purchases to secure supply-constrained infrastructure (thanks to AI boom) ahead of expected price increases. This redirection of budgets towards AI has been a topic that Karl Keirstead/team have been articulating as potential risk for some time (particularly for incumbent SaaS suppliers and IT Services companies), which sounds like a harbinger of commentary that could be further accentuated by other software, IT services and hardware-related companies as Q2 reporting season progresses that is sure to weigh on sentiment incrementally.
It only suprises the retarded

Early replies (first 3)

62480934
I'm doesnt even make anything anymore

62480935
>>62480918
And now, walls of cope from insects running the jeet infestations at other firms.

>David Vogt provides his initial thoughts on the IBM miss and these results suggest that enterprise IT spending pressures are hitting sooner than investors anticipated, leading to a revenue shortfall and non-GAAP EPS guidance of $2.93, below both expectations and consensus. The primary driver was weakness in IBM's zSeries mainframe cycle, which hurt its high-margin Transaction Processing (TP) business. While Red Hat delivered solid 11% constant-currency growth and recently acquired assets such as HashiCorp and Confluent performed well, these positives were overshadowed by a sharp decline in TP revenue, which appears to have fallen in the mid-teens year over year and represents nearly 30% of IBM's Software segment. As a result, investors are likely to reassess IBM's long-term software growth outlook, particularly for 2027 and beyond, as rising infrastructure costs and tightening IT budgets weigh on demand. These results reinforce concerns that stronger growth areas like Red Hat may not be sufficient to offset prolonged weakness in TP business, increasing pressure on IBM to pursue larger acquisitions or other growth initiatives to sustain its software growth trajectory remaining at neutral.
A 24% loss of value is a 'miss'. Ok david.

62480941
>>62480934
Well, definitely not now. But it was a white business, so it has to be trained by diversity.

Get it? TRAINED?!?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: PAIN. that's all that's in store for stinkies. pure unadulterated PAIN — 14 replies — 11.0/hr

OP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62475276
>>62474586
Pain is all they know and they relish it. They're Sirgays little buttboys, waiting with nervous anticipation with their overlord for BTC to increase in price then together screaming in agonizing pleasure as he forces searing hot dump after dump into their anal cavities, their greasy pudgy bodies convulsing uncontrollably. With each anon that jeers at their predicament their thirst for their lords girthy dumps grows, their grip on their ancient forgotten stacks tighten and they look up at the sky screaming "SIRGAY!!!! MORE!!!!"

62475282
>>62474586
>>62474608
>>62474699
>>62475276
Hello link bagholders how are you doing today?

62475442
>>62474608
Hello my stinky little linky, how many thousands of dollars have you been scammed out of?

62475861
PAIN

62478908
>>62474586
What happened to the misery and cuckoldery?

62479004
so what compels a person to baghold for 9 years?

62479044
>>62474586
PAIN and MISERY bump

62479106
>>62474608
Aren't LINK holders the ones who are mentally ill here?

62479140
>>62474586
Schwab anon was a literal prophet. He was fudding in 2020 and back then everyone thought he was a schizo freak because everyone believed link would go to $1000...

62479255
What doesnt kill makes you stronger

62480694
>>62479140
he was the only smart linkie, the rest are unironic shitskins or low IQ retards who regurgitate whatever CLG and ChainlinkLabs spams on Twitter. It's basically the Qanon of crypto

item.title: Everest MiCA CASP — 56 replies — 59.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Everest MiCA CASP

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-07-14 16:22 UTC

OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

Early replies (first 3)

62461998
>>62461988
I thought it would have mooned harder from the news. What gives

62462006
It is because we are meant to suffer as token holders just like the Linkies

62463131
>Unlisted from coingecko
Lmao!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62472466
>>62471613
The funniest shit is that >>62471100
was bragging about the price change from ATL, which was caused by a jeet hack few months ago. Until now Everest never officially acknowledge said hack, probably don't even file a report, and instead, pulled out liquidity so nobody else could sell.

Hence for some time, even a $100 buy/sell made a huge price impact. They added liquidity back last week and so far, any green candles you see on daily chart is carried by like 2 hodlers deep in sunk cost fallacy, as if Everest were the only way that could help them escape from the wage cage

62472472
>>62472466
Holy shit what a shitcoin. How can anyone hold this trash

62472532
lmao the everest tranny is back, back to shill your scam for more HRT?

62472534
>>62472466
The only reason why I don't have any sympathy for this retard is because he was shitting up the catalog with his samefag threads and shitty memes a couple years ago.

62472565
>>62472466
this just shows how corrupt EU is, they give MICA to literal scams with like 2 users

62472568
Lol Everbased sold and left years ago retards, the holders left now are based schizophrenics

62472588
>>62472565
Yet Binance couldn’t get it and is the most corrupt exchange in existence. Stupid idiot

62472601
>>62472588
yeah exactly

62472609
Brother he is calling you the stupid idiot lmfao my goodness

62472610
>>62472601
Thanks for agreeing with me that the EU doesn't give MICA out to scams

62472628
>>62472610
binance > evergayst

62472645
>>62471613
Kek this created some hard bagholders.

62472649
Bob was spot on regarding MiCA the last 5 years and now they have the entire EU in their hands. The 283 CASPs that cleared the bar are mostly exchanges. Everest holds the same passport but delivers identity, custody and a white labeled payment infrastructure across 27 states — rails for businesses, not another venue. Enjoy it and cry for next 5 years

62472755
>>62472649
5 more years :DDDDDDDDDDD

62472818
>>62461988
Everest didn’t randomly integrate Stellar — they built on it deliberately because Stellar is positioned to become the compliant public blockchain backbone for the tokenized economy, and Everest is engineering the regulated on-ramps/wallets/payments layer to capture it. Here’s the maximalist, everything-aligns thesis:
1. Strategic Positioning at the Perfect Intersection
• Everest = MiCA-licensed (EU passport), compliant custody, payments, fiat on/off-ramps, and now native Stellar wallet generation (BIP39 in EverWallet via SCF Kickstart Award).
• Stellar = Fast, cheap, compliance-native (clawbacks, authorized trustlines, etc.), with massive institutional validation via DTCC.
• The Kickstart Award isn’t just funding — it’s SDF officially stamping Everest as a key builder. This opens doors to deeper integration, more grants, co-marketing, and priority support as Stellar scales.
Everest becomes the regulated European gateway for Stellar flows: EU users/institutions access DTCC-tokenized assets (stocks, ETFs, Treasuries), stablecoins, and cross-border payments seamlessly through EverWallet.

62472822
>>62472818
DTCC connecting its tokenization service to Stellar (DTC assets live H1 2027) is one of the biggest TradFi-crypto bridges ever. We’re talking infrastructure for quadrillions in annual settlement volume potentially touching a public chain.
• Tokenized RWAs on Stellar need compliant wallets, custody, transfers, and EU distribution.
• Everest’s MiCA license + Stellar-native wallet = plug-and-play solution for European participants. No need for institutions to build everything themselves.
• Result: Explosive volume on Stellar higher XLM demand (fees, liquidity, bridges) + massive utility for Everest’s platform.
This isn’t payments-only anymore. It’s Everest sitting at the intersection of Wall Street tokenization and EU-regulated access.
3. Tokenized Economy Flywheel
• MiCA Advantage: Everest is already authorized for custody, transfers, etc. As MiCA matures and tokenized securities/stablecoins boom in Europe, Everest white-labels solutions for merchants, fintechs, and institutions.
• Wallet + Payments Moat: EverWallet with seamless Stellar (plus BTC/SOL roadmap) becomes a multi-chain compliant hub. Users generate Stellar addresses easily, hold tokenized assets, send remittances, swap, earn — all regulated.
• Network Effects: More assets on Stellar (DTCC + others) more users need compliant interfaces Everest captures fees, custody spreads, and ecosystem partnerships. SCF validation accelerates this.

62472827
>>62472822
• Stellar TVL and volume surge from DTCC flows XLM utility and price re-rate significantly (institutional adoption narrative goes parabolic).
• Everest, as an early regulated builder with SDF backing, sees user growth, revenue from services, and potential token ($ID) or valuation upside as the “Stellar-compliant layer in Europe.”
• Multi-chain expansion (already hinting BTC/SOL) positions them beyond one network while Stellar remains the core for real-world compliant assets.
• Timing: 2026–2027 window is when pilots go live. Everest’s Kickstart work is shipping now, giving them first-mover polish in wallets/UI.
Why They Chose Stellar “For a Reason”: Low costs + speed for payments/remittances, built-in compliance primitives perfect for regulated tokenized securities, and now direct DTCC pipeline. Ethereum is great for DeFi but heavier/costlier for high-volume settlement. Stellar is the efficient, institutional-friendly public chain.
In the super scenario: DTCC/Stellar becomes the standard for tokenized securities. Everest rides it as the go-to EU-compliant wallet/custody/payments layer. XLM reprices for infrastructure status. Everest scales into a major fintech player in the tokenized economy, with strong revenue, user base, and ecosystem gravity.
This is the aligned, non-random bet: regulation + infrastructure + timing + grants + institutional tailwinds. High conviction setup if the DTCC integration delivers.

62473294
>>62472818
>>62472822
>>62472827

62473379
>>62473294
Don’t forget to wear a mask

62473549
>dead shitchain

https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/may/27/tokenization-service-to-connect-with-stellar-public-blockchain-as-dtc-advances-multi-chain-strategy

62474220
SILENCE YOUR VOICE! ITS TUESDAY THERE ARE NO BITCHES OUT

62474779
When will token be on a bigger exchange?

62475324
Uniswap has more than enough liquidity, partnership with MiCA compliant exchange would be nice down the line too

62475402
>>62472818
>>62472822
>>62472827

62476783
>>62461988
Hope you like my fat dumps. I got 4 million more where that came from

62476859
>>62476783
its over for everestgays

62480107
New chapter in a long book my special friend Lenny

62480544
>>62461988
This is a mooner

62480654
lol @ the shameless attempts of keeping this thread alive. Every time it’s about to archive, some “new” anon comes in to bump it to the top. No one is buying your bags retard.

62480772
Keep coping buddy, ID will dildo in your face one day and then you are the clown

item.title: Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late. — 131 replies — 51.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Open thread • Replies: 131 • Updated: 2026-07-14 14:45 UTC

OP

Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Early replies (first 3)

62466535
They should just make corporate espionage legal in western countries at this point. The only people who are winning from the current laws are the Chinese

62466548
>>62466527
>state media says
lol

62466549
>>62466546
Show me one innovation from Japan.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62469825
>>62469777
>So fucking what?
None of this would happen without the $3 million investment. None of this. The guy was extremely lucky in this regard that he had such an opportunity.

62469856
it's using a net lmao

62469886
>>62466527
Did China steal blueprints from SpaceX?

62470354
>>62469886
Long March 10B is about as different from Falcon 9 as a rocket can get while still being a rocket that does propulsive landing. Cable catch, dual fuel, seven engined booster, closed cycle engines, oxidizer-rich preburner, etc.

62470360
>>62466535
Corporate espionage happens all the time regardless of its legality and there's zero chance that the Chinks aren't just being used by Western alphabet agencies. The moment they come up with, test, build or create anything that is a legitimate threat you can expect everyone involved to suicide by two shots to the back of the head or for the entire complex to have a rocket accident and get blown up in the explosion. People do not share power, especially not over international borders.

>>62466548
Also this. You can generally assume that everything China ever says is just lies projected to a face-saving culture. Its for domestic consumption, not for us to speculate about.

62470370
Buy SPCX, the prices won’t get much lower than this. You don’t want to be an earth bagholder, do you?

62470418
>>62470360
>The moment they come up with, test, build or create anything that is a legitimate threat you can expect everyone involved to suicide by two shots to the back of the head or for the entire complex to have a rocket accident and get blown up in the explosion

They should have done that before Chinese satellites ruined Trump's Iran adventure by providing Iran with the exact locations in real time of air defenses, aircraft parking spots and other assets in the region, as well as providing damage assessment so Iran didn't waste missiles on an already destroyed target

62470662
>>62466603
>The one really great thing about this is that maybe now China will stop crashing boosters onto their own citizens' villages.
It's due to the fact their spaceport is so far inland it's impossible to not drop on it's citizens. Russia's fall over Siberia, while USA shoots over oceans.

62471032
>>62466603
>>62470662
Rockets launched from Wenchang fall into the sea, not onto populated areas. It's the rockets launched from Xichang that fall onto populated areas. This is because the location for Xichang spaceport was chosen during the Cold War when security from military strike was considered very important. There are no unpopulated in the Chinese interior at the low latitudes from which you can launch to geosynchronous orbit. Xichang spaceport will probably be closed within 5 years, or at most 10 years.

62471086
>>62470662
only an issue because of hypergolic fuels
otherwise its just a random debris cloud

62471098
>>62466590
>regional conflict in eastern yurop
we're talking about big picture stuff here, anon. no one cares about the vatnik-gopnik war

62471197
>>62468297
>>62468335
>noo you HAVE to write in correct english and use common english saying correctly for us to trust you!
your country is owned by jews who speak your language only because it's convenient to them. the universal language of the future will be chinese, while the US will be speaking spanish in the next decades, so get used to it, RETARD.

62471338
>>62467458
>>62467520
so starship isn't a factor? the servers in space thing is not priced in?

62471395
>>62466527
Kek, of course it looks just like Elon's. If China didnt have the US to steal from theys be an absolute disaster failure state.

62471694
>>62471338
>the servers in space thing
Complete nonsense, zero chance of turning a profit, ever, without miraculous breakthroughs in materials science. I work as a cloud engineer in the space industry, I mange enormous numbers of servers that are used by an entire subdivision of our company that are employed for the sole task of determining if a satellite's design is going cause it to experience runaway heat issues and fail. The idea of putting racks of fucking GPUs of all things into LEO is what we would come up with if we wanted to create a parody of what a clueless pleb would try to pitch to investors. Its going to be another full self-driving mode, robotaxi, robovan (remember that one?), humanoid robots, and the mission to mars. Its a fantasy, its not happening. He'll probably try, no doubt, and it will work for a month or so, stock will pump massively, and then fail.

62471784
>>62469825
faggot, do you think nobody else would have invented the blue led?

62471789
>>62466535
what makes you think the US havent already been doing this for decades at this point in supposedly 'allied' western countries like japan and s korea? long before the chinese ever became a serious threat

62471902
>>62471694
>zero chance of turning a profit
anon, we are not talking about reality or about what YOU think about it. and investors think "oh, they made a reusable rocket, they surely have enough engineering knowledge or ingenuity to pull it off!", so people invest based on assumptions and fantasies rather than on reality. just look at the AI bubble...

62471927
>>62471784
He wouldn't. He wouldn't invent anything without the funding. You seem to be very angry about this.

62471932
>>62471927
who cares who does it, why do you think it matters and why do you think i care about that one guy?

62471938
>>62471932
Try to read the posts you're replying to you fucking retard

62471973
>>62471694
>turning a profit
why would you think this is important, bigger companies have survived for longer without profit, your small minded thinking does not work at this scale

62472725
>>62471395
>muh it's also a cylinder so it has to be a carbon copy
Long March 10B is about as different from Falcon 9 as a rocket can be. Different engine count, different propellants, different engine combustion cycle, different preburner, different recovery method.

62472738
>>62466624
>(Elon's never going to sell his half of the company.)
The fat arsehole has preferential stock.
You'd still need to suck his dick if you bought 80% of this fucking garbage.

62474107
spcx is gonna go down 20% at open because of this stupid FUD

62475125
spcx is going to 30 because they are about to float 400x the amount of shares lmao
u guys are complete retards

62476545
Current price: $138.58

Only like $3 over the IPO price and below the market open price.

This with most of the shares still locked up. I think I showed a chart and it should show that 60% of shares will be unlocked by October. That is a 12x increase in float. IMO this stock is either going down and trading sideways. If you like SpaceX, wait for a better entry point. Maybe around $90.

62480129
/biz/ needs country flags

62480305
>spacex
>p/s of over a hundred
>2 trillion evaluation
>runs at a loss
Seems like a wonderful shorting opportunity. why shouldn't i go balls deep in bear etf's on the instrument?

62480336
>>62470360
>muttjeet powerfantasy

item.title: the betrayal was real — 35 replies — 32.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
the betrayal was real

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-07-14 16:14 UTC

OP

This man has financially raped me over the last 6 years. I might have to get a job if LINK doesn't pump to triple digits by 2028

Early replies (first 3)

62475075
>>62475029
The betrayal was real indeed, but it came from bitcoin

62475079
>>62475075
not buying your russian jeetcoin, negro

62475085
He never promised you anything. You agreed to buy the token to fund his corporation, with no promise of any returns.

Dont blame him for the story you made up inside your own head

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62475116
>>62475085
He promised becoming the dominant oracle and the cornerstone of crypto adoption.
He delivered this in spades.

He also promised the exclusive use of the token for node payments.
He also delivered this.

62476551
>>62475075
You are low IQ or deep-coping. The market is downstream macro and global liquidity events exogenous of crypto which feed into it but you are a RETARD if you think pricing mechanics don't change functionally, fundamentally on the basis of real utility being allowed and used. We are not always going to be a random speculative niche, and XRP specifically is well situated for this. It doesn't mean clarity will send the price up the second it is signed but it will absolutely be a necessary doorway to the kinds of change we need to have real systems migrate so they don't get fucked in a future admin agency guidance and fed direction etc. and the fact it is happening already has banks preparing so they are not late to the ones that do. You have a point very very narrowly but are wrong in the grander sense and in the specific sense about price not being affected by news as described by the two things mentioned even using your own example of the lawsuit both at the launch and resolution of it. You are blackpilled by uncritical impatience and it is affecting your analysis.

62476961
>>62475085
Yeah, Sergey was very careful to not shill the token ever. His project has been and will be successful without it. Fuck all the autistic retards who thought they were smarter than "normies" and that they would be rich by buying some stupid unknown magic coin without ever touching grass. Fucking hate these board spammers who still dont move on.

62477071
>>62475029
>triple digits
sir, I have bad news, the pool was redeemed

62477420
>>62476961
staking = shilling
they called the yield "rewards" for this reason, as a thin legal veneer. it is, however, now a security

62477941
>>62475029
He literally never once claimed that the token would go up in price. You came up with that lore yourselves in 2018

62477977
>>62477941
He did specify that the ideal price would be that of the price of a cup of coffee.

What's the average US price for a cup of coffee again? Like $4-5?

62478081
>>62475116
he didn't deliver shit. his "oracles" are permissioned insiders that relay prices from binance. there is literally no reason to trust chainlink over your own oracle, as demonstrated by hyperliquid, because all price data in this shit market comes from the same place. no one has adopted crypto, defi is dying, and there are countless alternatives to chainlink.

62479194
>>62478081
>there is literally no reason to trust chainlink over your own oracle
riiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiight, pic very related

>all price data in this shit market comes from the same place
It really really really really does not.

>as demonstrated by hyperliquid
Hyperliquid's internal oracle has had literally hundreds of failures.
Ask your local AI if you don't believe me.

That's because its basic architecture is a very basic, small-scale, and overall shitty system that other projects only use as a tertiary backup.

62479198
>>62475029
Rip my cousin Ray. Sergay beat Ray. Can you believe that shit nigger?

62479343
>>62479194
>saaaaaar those basterds Bitcoin whales are the reason my village is hungry

62479429
>>62479343
That pic isn't about the suppression.

62479490
>>62475029
Bottom signal

62479787
>>62479194
there is no "internal oracle" on Hyperliquid, the validators pull data from CEX APIs just like Chainlink does. The validator set is the DON and it is virtually identical to how Chainlink price feeds work. Anyone, absolutely anyone, could set up the exact same shit for their chain, and pull the exact same data from the exact same places. The only question is whether users trust "the brand", when under the hood it's all the same shit. Chainlink showed on October 10 that they are NOT anymore trustworthy than any other oracle, and will just report whatever Binance says the price is

62479810
Did you even say thank you?

62479818
>>62479787
>there is no "internal oracle" on Hyperliquid
>the validators pull data from CEX APIs just like Chainlink does
That's what makes those validators oracles.

>The validator set is the DON and it is virtually identical to how Chainlink price feeds work.
Then why did Hyperliquid's oracle have so many failures, while Chainlink had none?

And in the case of the Jelly exploit, Hyperliquid even intervened manually so you know the whole "validators = oracles" setup allows for Hyperliquid to manually manipulate their system.

>Anyone, absolutely anyone, could set up the exact same shit for their chain
And they'll have tons of failures, just like Hyperliquid and all the others that tried.

>Chainlink showed on October 10 that they are NOT anymore trustworthy than any other oracle, and will just report whatever Binance says the price is
Obviously, when that's the actual spot price.

62479865
>>62479818
>Obviously, when that's the actual spot price.

>all prices drop 90% in less than 15 minutes
>Binance even states that they were the cause of the event
>Chainlink says nothing, does nothing

>and in the case of the Jelly exploit
which, yet again, was a direct attack by Binance, something Chainlink apparently cannot prevent and in fact willingly assists. So there is literally no reason to trust or use them, when every Chainlink DON is also permissioned, centralized, and an agent of a malicious centralized party

62479878
>>62479818
>Obviously, when that's the actual spot price.
Wasnt Binances spot prices way off from the rest of the market on a lot of pairs? Isn't that the kind of issue that decentralized oracles are supposed to solve?

62479987
I'm a black man and I can smell a scam from a mile away. Chainlink is a textbook ponzi scheme

62480313
>>62479865
>>62479878
You're gonna have to actually tell us what the fuck you're talking about.
Chainlink was extremely accurate on the price of BTC, ETH, and even USDe, one of the lynchpins of the 10/10 crash.

62480387
>>62479865
>>62479878
See pic.
https://llamarisk.com/research/when-pricing-breaks-usde

USDe lost its peg on Binance alone, dropping to $0.65 only there.
Pyth's USDe price dropped 4.3% as a result.
Chainlink's USDe price only dropped 0.64% however, well within the margins of normal operational safety even.

If anything Chainlink helped prevent much worse on 10/10.

Also, even if Chainlink DID fail, there would be no manual clawback like the one that happened on Hyperliquid.
Because Chainlink does not control the nodes like Hyperliquid does, and it DEFINITELY does not control the blockchain like Hyperliquid also does.

62480445
>>62480313
>>62480387
It doesn't matter that Chainlink "accurately reported" what Binance said the price was. Do you understand that defi is not possible if a centralized party can just declare what the prices are? Massive defi contagion was caused by a centralized party manipulating prices. I recall when this was a foundational problem, which decentralized oracles were created to fight. 10/10 showed that Chainlink is either unable or unwilling to stop this kind of attack. No oracle on the market is, because as we know, every oracle sources their pricing data from Binance and no oracle service is willing to oppose centralized exchanges. 10/10 was the biggest manipulation event in crypto's history, bar none, which should have sparked a massive movement demanding more resilient pricing mechanisms. Instead there was total silence, and quite frankly, implicit complicity in what occurred

62480513
>>62476551
Its so weird when legit genius level people type and they use schoolyard insults but everything else is academic language.

62480526
>>62480445
>It doesn't matter that Chainlink "accurately reported" what Binance said the price was.
t. illiterate

USDe on Binance: $0.65
USDe price on Chainlink: $0.9935

>Do you understand that defi is not possible if a centralized party can just declare what the prices are?
Chainlink is decentralized, Hyperliquid isn't.

Chainlink has tons of verifiably independent nodes, Hyperliquid has 24 and most of them are likely run by the team itself (see the Jelly clawback).

Also, in the case of Hyperliquid, the price oracles are also the blockchain nodes; so if one is compromised then so is the other.

Which is why Hyperliquid has had a massive list of failures and exploits (and manual clawbacks), and Chainlink has had none.

62480678
>>62480526
Hyperliquid made investors rich
Chainlink made investors poor

anything else is cope, you're embarassing yourself

62480723
>>62480678
>Hyperliquid wasn't suppressed

ya I know

62480725
>>62480723
>life isn't fair I was supposed to be rich!!!
tough luck

62480729
>>62480725
>active suppression
>"luck"

62480735
>>62480729
not my problem baggie

62480739
>>62480735
nobody said it was

item.title: The DTCC news are a nothing burger for Link — 14 replies — 22.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
The DTCC news are a nothing burger for Link

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-07-14 16:42 UTC

OP

This is just a limited run, so don't be shocked if the price dumps. The Clarity Act hasn't been passed yet, and staking still hasn't been released.

Early replies (first 3)

62479296
I’m ready to fuck

62479350
>>62479296
you have had that cage on for so long can you even get erect anymore

62479357
>>62479295
Will Linkies unironically kill themseleves when DTCC goes live and Chainlink crashes to $5? Gosh that would be hilarious.

Imagine a bunch of third worlders and schizo firsties roping because they got scammed by a Russian conman.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62479513
Whats interesting is that they’ve achieved all the things everyone else promised. It’s like it’s the only thing worth holding because everything else is a huge scam.

62479790
>>62479513
Yup which is why I'm all in but even so I still don't think we'll hit God candles until staking and the clarity act passes. I just buy weekly and ignore the news and fuddies.

62479817
>>62479357
>calling white men firsties
how do I know you're brown and live in some shithole shack?

62480137
>>62479357
Projecting again are we, fudcuck?
Quick! You better go rush to doxx a linkie again. Hopefully you'll have more luck than finding a white STEM degree holder this time lol

62480294
>>62479513
>It’s like it’s the only thing worth holding because everything else is a huge scam.

lol! from the guy who just missed the last bullrun while down 80%

what exactly are you even holding?

62480300
>>62479790
>until staking and the clarity act passes

what specifically in the current version of the clarity do you think causes upward price action?

62480334
>>62479817
this lol

62480631
>>62480137
>>62479817
how many rupees is ChainlinkLabs paying you? Hope its worth it shitskins

62480673
>>62479790
This is mainly to find the correct bps per mil transactions

4.67 CCIP BPS is not what institutions will be paying, but the security angle is still there so we could see 0.02-0.07 per mil

DTCC does 2T a day btw

62480850
>>62480300
The Clarity Act is important because it would provide a much clearer regulatory framework for cryptocurrency and establish clearer rules for the industry. That legal certainty is one of the biggest reasons many financial institutions have been cautious about fully embracing blockchain technology and tokenizing real-world assets. Especially since you see so many scams going on, and those influencers never see the inside of a jail cell. It makes it harder for businesses and banks to take crypto seriously and makes them hesitant because if the SEC decides to act against them, there are no clear rules to protect them from liability. Companies managing billions of dollars aren’t going to want to invest in new tech because, again, there are no clear rules. If the act passes then more institutions will feel comfortable using blockchain technology.

62480856
>>62480850
>>62480300
Banks and governments aren’t going to use shitcoins, they're going to build their own private chains. How will they interact with other banks that use their own private chains tho? Chainlink. There is no real competition to them. Look at all the others that promote interoperability and get hacked *cough wormhole. Swift has worked with Chainlink multiple times and has even attended their SmartCon, and invited Sergey to SIBOS. Nigga, Sirgay is the only one related to crypto to even attend Sibos. For any brain-dead anons, Sibos isn’t just a regular conference. It's arguably the largest banking conference in the world, attended by thousands of representatives from banks, central banks, market infrastructures, payment providers, and asset managers. If you want to use Chainlink’s services like ccip or cre then you need the token. Don’t have the token? Well they can buy it for you or convert it and charge you at a premium. Right now not a lot of companies are using the services because crypto is a joke and chainlink is using their own war chest of tokens instead of buying it off the market so there is no real demand for tokens FOR NOW. Chainlink is such an obvious bet if you think crypto will ever be adopted. Fuddies are successful because the price is horrible right now and has been for years. I'm still buying Chainlink with the meager savings I have, but even I know that this tokenization will probably do jack shit for the price.

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 119 replies — 20.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 119 • Updated: 2026-07-14 11:30 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62472069
>>62466457
This will keep happening. Link won't re-enter the top 5 until btc goes to 35k

62472415
>>62472062
I swear we had this conversation before. The BTC network is inflationary but all new BTC that enters the market comes from the miners and they're the ones who put a price tag on it. They'll most definitely factor in the cost of operations into the coin. New Link that enters circulation comes not only from node operators selling but Chainlink Labs themselves selling tokens. More importantly, access to the node operators is supposed to be gatekept by Link tokens, but CLL keeps giving people outs to access the network without buying the Link token by paying in other currencies and CLL sits on their laurels and decides when and how much off their off chain revenue should be converted to Link from the markets ($1M a week). Stop being dishonest about the Link token.

62472614
>>62472415
>miners decide the price of BTC
You're legitimately retarded.

62472624
>>62472415
>CLL keeps giving people outs to access the network without buying the Link token
If users don't have to "buy" the Link token, they still have to "pay for" the token.
Just like if you pay for a package delivery, you're not "buying" the gasoline for the delivery truck, but you are paying for it.

62472630
>>62472062
>>62466927
>Some anon did the math in another thread;
Link to thread?

62472819
>>62467960
Obviously I meant for us to track it

62472871
>>62472614
Point exactly what I said about BTC and plug both it and your reinterpretation into ChatGPT.

>>62472624
>Just like if you pay for a package delivery, you're not "buying" the gasoline for the delivery truck, but you are paying for it.
The native token fees for CCIP are sitting in a contract and have yet to be converted to Link (PA version 2 is said to automate this). CLL converts offchain revenue at $1M a week where we have no idea what percentage of this is being converted in their offchain revenue.

You are theoretically correct about having to cover the costs of gas when buying X at the grocery store but in this case, not even the delivery trucks are buying gas with the payments made. The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market. Hence why the market price of gas isn't going up.

62472938
>>62472871
>exactly what I said about BTC
Here's exactly what you said: "all new BTC that enters the market comes from the miners and they're the ones who put a price tag on it"

You're a fucking retard who thinks miners determine the price of BTC.

>The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market.
Holy shit yes they are.

62473105
>>62472871
>The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market.
yeah this is definitely wrong lmao

62473264
>>62473105
That's exactly how all crypto inflationary rewards work.
For example: 95% of Bitcoin mining rewards are inflationary, and only like 5% have to be bought by users from the open market.

And in the case of Link, much of it is being bought on the open market via payment abstraction.

You cripples really need to learn how to sing a new tune.

62473278
>>62473264
No wonder you get grifted.
You dont understand supply and demand, how price is determined and that btc "inflation" =/= 8% supply increase aka "strategic unlocks"

62473280
>>62473278
No, you don't understand those things.

62474448
>>62472938
And what did I say after that?
>>62473105
>Holy shit yes they are.
>yeah this is definitely wrong lmao
What, the $1M a week Reserve buyback? The CCIP fee tokens stuck in a contract that have yet to be converted to Link? Realize when you give these fucking bullshit "technically..." answers, if you were right, then you would need to contend to why the price action is fucking shit. Why Chainlink is realizing the goals it was aiming to achieve for over 10 years in the release of DTCC's and SWIFT's DLT platforms and yet the price is $8. Why Chainlink is onboarding over $7B in protocol value to CCIP and the price is still $8. Hundreds of millions of dollars of revenue generated and still $8.

And notice you never directly addressed my points about $LINK being decoupled from the network. You keep making inaccurate comparisons.

62474875
>>62474448
>And what did I say after that?
After that you said "They'll most definitely factor in the cost of operations into the coin".
Which further proves that you thought miners determined the price of Bitcoin.

You're such a massive retard lmao.

>if you were right, then you would need to contend to why the price action is fucking shit
Two reasons:
1) crypto adoption as a whole is still insignificant, and all crypto valuation so far is speculative instead of functional
2) this >>62466457 >>62467657 >>62468805

62474886
>>62467048
whats stopping them from being paid in link then immediately selling that link for something else

62474889
>>62474886
lmao what's stopping literally any crypto miner/node on any L1 from doing that?

Why do link fuddies always have to be so explicit about being absolutely clueless on crypto?

62474913
>>62474448
well, i'll give you this pal, i did read, but i am, in fact, never selling.

62476934
>>62474889
because mined crypto has intrinsic value of the energy spent to create it, or revenue share or buybacks.

62476944
>>62476934
>mined crypto has intrinsic value of the energy spent to create it
please tell me you're only joking

62477003
This idea that link/xrp cucks have that banks will all of a sudden start buying mass amount of their respective shitcoins is truly a fascinating mass psychoses to behold. This board is basically an insane asylum at this point between the xrp and link spam.

62477008
>>62476944
the market must be a comedian too because that joke is told in every link pair chart

62477021
>>62477003
If banks want to use Chainlink (and they do), they have to pay for the work Chainlink nodes do, which is denominated and paid exclusively in Link tokens.

62477094
>>62474875
>After that you said "They'll most definitely factor in the cost of operations into the coin".
>Which further proves that you thought miners determined the price of Bitcoin.

Anon, you're calling me a retard for saying a miner, someone running operations would sell bitcoins at a loss. What happens to a business that doesn't make a profit? What do you think goes into a the mind of a miner received BTC out of the coin base and is about it sell it. You think he's gonna say "hmm, I rely on this to keep my operations running, I should sell it for lower than what it takes to keep the lights running"?

>>62474875
>1) crypto adoption as a whole is still insignificant, and all crypto valuation so far is speculative instead of functional
But Chainlink's revenue is far from insignificant and they gloat about generating hundreds of millions of dollars in revenue generated. Link has always been suppressed. However, can you show that $Link has been battling it out and contributing to fighting against the suppression in contributing to its price? For reasons I previously mentioned multiple times that you refuse to directly address, CLL is sabotaging its own internet funny money by giving customers like myself an avenue to bypass it and pay in USD. And only a meager and unknown percentage has been traceably converted to Link between the Reserve, SVR, and CCIP.

>>62474913
no conflicts between not selling and being realistic about the current tokenonmics till staking releases.

62477138
>>62477021
Its a flat fee in dollars, converted to link, paid to node operators, sold for dollars. Its an unneeded hop.

62477152
>>62477094
You didn't say "miners don't want to sell at a loss", you said "miners are the ones who put a price tag on BTC".

>>62477138
>Its an unneeded hop
It really isn't. For the simplicity and uniformity of the basic infrastructure alone it makes perfect sense to use a single token.
And even notorious Link fudder Vitalik admits the economic (dis)incentive aspect makes a token needed as well.

62477191
>>62477152
>And even notorious Link fudder Vitalik
He is jelly , he fiddled with the whole proof of work thing while he could have printed out of thin air and just dumped annualy in the form of token unlocks.

The only good thing with the link conversion/sale is the node operators can sell it for thei local jeetcoin currency.

62477445
>>62477152
>You didn't say "miners don't want to sell at a loss", you said "miners are the ones who put a price tag on BTC".

62477487
>>62477445
If you think those are the same thing I can see why you are frustrated with someone pointing out the error to begin with.

The market decides the price. Miners have 2 options:

1. Sell now to cover costs
2. Eat the costs and wait for the market to improve

Neither scenario is them setting the price. Scenario 2 is miners speculating with coins the same way any market participant does, except their tokens were previously uncirculating.

62479611
>>62477445
Those two statements are WORLDS apart.

You are seriously stupid, I want you to know that so maybe in the future you won't post as much out of embarrassment (slim chance, I know).
